title: Migrate from IRSA to EKS Pod Identity
scenario: Your `s3-reader` ServiceAccount uses IRSA via the `eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn` annotation. AWS now recommends EKS Pod Identity — it doesn't require OIDC thumbprint rotation, supports cross-account IAM without trust policy edits, and simplifies IAM role reuse. Migrate: remove the IRSA annotation and add the Pod Identity annotation.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: s3-reader
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/S3ReaderRole

hints.text: Remove `eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn` and replace it with `eks.amazonaws.com/pod-identity-association`, keeping the same role ARN value.
Pod Identity uses a separate EKS API (`eks:CreatePodIdentityAssociation`) to link IAM roles to service accounts — no OIDC provider needed in the trust policy.
The annotation key changes from `eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn` to `eks.amazonaws.com/pod-identity-association`. The value (the ARN) stays the same.

solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: s3-reader
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    eks.amazonaws.com/pod-identity-association: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/S3ReaderRole
